title: Deyeuxia swallenii
intro: Deyeuxia swallenii is a species of grass, specifically a type of perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Bioactive constituents of Deyeuxia swallenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and extracts, allowing for its bioactive compounds to be utilized for therapeutic purposes.
taxonomy: Deyeuxia swallenii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Poaceae. This classification places it within the monotypic genus Deyeuxia. Further classification within the family Poaceae groups it as a member of the subfamily Pooideae. Its classification also groups it with other grasses, specifically within the tribe Poeae. Deyeuxia swallenii is native to the mountainous regions of Central and South America.
